intro: Instagram Reel Comment Scraper pulls every top-level comment from a public Instagram Reel — commenter username, comment text, likes, and timestamp — without logging into Instagram or touching a single cookie. It's built for anyone who needs to read what an audience is actually saying under a Reel, not just how many likes or views it got.
useCases: Run campaign or product-launch comments through a sentiment model to see how an audience really reacted.
Pull comments from a competitor's Reels to see what their audience complains about or asks for.
Check whether an influencer's comment section looks like genuine engagement before a partnership.
Build a labeled dataset of public comment text for NLP or research work.
Export a brand's Reel comments for community management review outside the Instagram app.

faq.q: Do I need an Instagram account to use this?
faq.a: No. It only works on public Reels and never requires a login, cookies, or an Instagram account.

faq.q: Can it scrape private reels or posts that aren't Reels?
faq.a: No — only publicly visible Reels are supported. It's built and tested for /reel/, /reels/, and /p/ links, since Instagram uses all three interchangeably for Reels.

faq.q: Does it fetch comment replies, or only top-level comments?
faq.a: Only top-level comments in this version. Each comment includes a child_comment_count so you know how many replies exist, but the replies themselves aren't extracted yet.

faq.q: How much does it cost to run?
faq.a: The actor itself is free — every result is priced at $0. You only pay Apify's standard platform compute cost for the run.

faq.q: Can I extract comments from multiple Reels in one run?
faq.a: Yes. Add multiple URLs to the input list; each is processed independently, so one failed or deleted Reel doesn't stop the others.
